title: Expose semantic memory search as a tool — wraps existing T-080 embedding engine
component: tools/tools_memory.py (new memory_search_semantic method + TOOLS entry), agent/modes.py (add to god allowlist)
current_state: T-080 shipped Gemini embeddings + cosine similarity for L2 duplicate detection (memory/semantic_dedup.py). The engine works (16/16 tests pass). But the planner can't call it for retrieval — semantic similarity only fires inside the write-path dedup check. Result: 'what did we decide about caching' misses an L2 row titled 'Redis Migration ADR' because BM25 + entity matching share zero tokens.
structural_problem: Pi has 3 search layers (obsidian_search file grep, memory_read BM25+entity, semantic_dedup cosine) but only 2 are tools the planner sees. The most powerful one is internal-only. Adding it as a tool is a 1-ToolSpec entry in the new registry — proves the value of T-083 R2.1.
target_state: memory_search_semantic(query, limit=5, threshold=0.5) registered as a ToolSpec in tools/tools_memory.py::TOOLS. Wraps get_embedding() + cosine_similarity() from memory.semantic_dedup. Pulls all L2 rows with stored embeddings (metadata.embedding from T-080), scores each, returns top N >= threshold. Graceful degrade when GEMINI_API_KEY missing or no embeddings stored.
files_affected: testing/test_semantic_dedup.py (extend with retrieval test, or new file)
success_criteria: memory_search_semantic('caching decisions', limit=3) returns L2 rows by cosine descending
Returns [] gracefully when GEMINI_API_KEY missing (no exception)
Returns [] when no L2 rows have stored embeddings (pre-T-080 vault)
ToolSpec registered with success_predicate handling empty results vs errors
Added to god ModeConfig tool_allowlist (read-only, no privacy leak)
risk_notes: Low risk. New tool, no replacement. Cost per call ~$0.00002 (Gemini embed). T-080 engine already battle-tested via 16-test suite.
migration_plan: 1. Add memory_search_semantic() method to MemoryTools (queries supabase L2 where metadata.embedding exists, computes cosine vs query embedding, returns top N)
2. Add _handle_memory_search_semantic to TOOLS section of tools_memory.py
4. Add 'memory_search_semantic' to god tool_allowlist in agent/modes.py
5. Smoke test: mock supabase rows with known embeddings, assert top-N ordering matches expected cosine ranks
6. verify.py PASS, then close as part of T-096 vault-polish sweep or its own solution
